abstract:
Abstract
                  
                    Research Summary
                    The emergence of large language models (LLMs) offers new opportunities for AI integration in research, particularly for data annotation and text classification. However, researchers lack guidance on implementation best practices, as the benefits and risks of these tools remain poorly understood. We develop a foundational framework for effective LLM implementation in management research, providing structured guidance on key decisions throughout the research process. We illustrate this framework through an empirical application: classifying sustainability claims in crowdfunding projects. While LLMs can match or exceed traditional methods' performance at lower cost, we find that variations in prompt design can significantly affect results and downstream analyses. We develop procedures for sensitivity analysis and provide detailed documentation to help researchers implement robustness while maintaining methodological integrity.
                  
                  
                    Managerial Summary
                    Large language models (LLMs) offer powerful new tools for business research, especially for analyzing and categorizing text data. However, managers and researchers lack clear guidance on how to use these tools effectively and reliably. This study creates a practical framework for implementing LLMs in management research, covering key decisions from model selection to result validation. Using a real‐world example of analyzing sustainability claims in crowdfunding campaigns, we demonstrate that LLMs can match traditional methods while being faster and cheaper. However, small changes in how you instruct the AI can significantly alter results and business conclusions. We provide systematic procedures for testing result reliability and offer practical tools to help managers implement AI‐powered analysis while maintaining rigorous standards and avoiding misleading findings.

ob.ai_reason: LLM大语言模型在管理研究数据标注中的应用指南，核心AI议题
ob.tldr: 研究针对管理学领域使用大语言模型（LLM）进行数据标注提供实践指南。通过对众筹项目可持续发展声明分类的实证应用，发现LLM可低成本达到或超越传统方法，但提示词设计差异会显著影响结果。研究提出敏感性分析程序和详细文档规范，为研究者提供兼顾稳健性与方法论完整性的实现路径。

title:
The use of
                    <scp>LLMs</scp>
                    to annotate data in management research: Foundational guidelines and warnings
title_zh: 管理研究中LLMs数据标注的应用：基础指南与风险警示
